assertion_results.text: Documentation includes a dedicated Error Responses section explaining the standard error format
assertion_results.evidence: Error Handling section at bottom shows the standard error format with code and message fields

assertion_results.text: The standard error response schema is documented with fields: code, message, and details
assertion_results.evidence: Error format shows code and message but omits the 'details' field which is present in the source code's AppError class

assertion_results.text: Each endpoint documents all possible error status codes (400, 404, 409, 422, 429, 500)
assertion_results.evidence: Only 404 and 409 mentioned on individual endpoints. No 422, 429, or 500 per-endpoint. No comprehensive error listing.

assertion_results.text: Validation errors (400/422) include details about which fields failed and why
assertion_results.evidence: No validation error details documented. No field-level error information.

assertion_results.text: Custom error classes (NotFoundError, ConflictError) are mapped to their HTTP status codes
assertion_results.evidence: Error section mentions VALIDATION_ERROR (422), NOT_FOUND (404), CONFLICT (409) but doesn't map the class names or cover all custom classes (RateLimitError, UnauthorizedError, ForbiddenError missing)

assertion_results.text: Zod schema validation rules are reflected in parameter constraints (min, max, regex, enum)
assertion_results.evidence: POST endpoint lists required/optional fields but no constraints from Zod schemas (no min/max lengths, no regex for SKU, no enum values for category)

assertion_results.text: At least one error response example is shown per endpoint with realistic error data
assertion_results.evidence: No error response examples shown for any endpoint. Only inline text like 'Returns 404 if not found'.

assertion_results.text: The OpenAPI spec includes error response schemas in the components section
assertion_results.evidence: No OpenAPI JSON spec was produced
